text: ITALY BREAKS RANKS: PM Meloni suspends two-decade defense pact with Israel. FM Tajani: 'unacceptable attacks' on civilians. Israel summons ambassador. Third bilateral protest. Kallas: 'gravest violation of international law since WWII.' EU sanctions 19 Iranian officials. Lavrov arrives Beijing to coordinate with Wang Yi. Europe fragmenting — between opposing Israel AND sanctioning Iran simultaneously.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Haaretz confirmed Italy. EEAS confirmed Kallas. RFE/RL confirmed Lavrov-Beijing.

text: Day 46 PM roundup: Oil plunges Brent $94.87 WTI $91.42 on talk hopes. BLOCKADE DAY 1: 6 ships U-turned. Italy suspends defense pact. Hezbollah jam-proof drone hits 20+ targets. IDF 'Op Silver Plough' buffer zone. Russia evacuates Bushehr. Barnea: 'regime change.' Iran UN letter: 'gross violation.' IEA: 10.1M bpd lost. IMF: 3.1% growth. Kallas: 'gravest since WWII.' Lavrov-Beijing. Chelsea Manning arrested. Gas $4.118.
verificationNote: Compiled from CNBC, Trading Economics, Bloomberg, Al Jazeera, Haaretz, Middle East Monitor, Kurdistan24, Moscow Times, PressTV, PBS, Newsweek, AAA.

text: SANCTIONS ESCALATION: Bloomberg reports US Treasury will NOT renew 30-day Iran oil waiver when it expires April 19. ~140M barrels stranded Iranian oil authorization ends. Russia oil waiver also expired April 11. India faces double hit — lost 3M bpd from Hormuz + 1.5M bpd Russian crude now restricted. This is 'full force Economic Fury.' China gets 97.6% of Iran's oil exports — Beijing is the real target here.
verificationNote: Bloomberg, Reuters via Investing.com, CNBC confirmed waiver expiry and India double hit.

text: HISTORIC: First Israel-Lebanon direct talks since 1993 at Rubio's State Dept. Both sides agree to further negotiations. BUT Hezbollah's Qassem torpedoes: 'futile, surrender.' Wafiq Safa: 'will not abide by any agreements.' Israeli Amb. Leiter: 'Don't want French near this.' Haaretz: Netanyahu using talks to buy time for Bint Jbeil. Diplomacy vs. Hezbollah veto power — the core Lebanon dilemma.
verificationNote: NPR, State Department, Axios, Haaretz, Democracy Now confirmed talks and rejections.

text: MARKETS: S&P 500 closes at 6,967 (+1.18%) — erases ALL Iran war losses. Nasdaq +1.96% to 23,639. Rally driven by Iran talk optimism despite bad PPI. Oil settles Brent $94.79, WTI $91.28 — afternoon session extends decline. 10Y yield 4.248%. Markets betting on diplomatic resolution before April 21 ceasefire expiry. But sanctions waiver expiring + blockade Day 2 = mixed signals.
verificationNote: CNBC, Motley Fool, Yahoo Finance confirmed market data. TheStreet confirmed close.

text: Haaretz INVESTIGATION: 1 in 4 Iranian missiles penetrated Israel's air defenses in the last week of the 40-day war. Cluster missiles caused significant damage. Intercept rates degraded sharply from early war performance. This contradicts the 'impenetrable Iron Dome' narrative. Also: IRGC Navy warns any military vessel approaching Hormuz = ceasefire violation. Rhetoric escalating.
verificationNote: Haaretz investigation confirmed. Islam Times confirmed IRGC warning.

text: Xi Jinping's FIRST personal statement since war began: 'world must not revert to law of the jungle.' 4 proposals for Middle East peace to Abu Dhabi Crown Prince. Also told Spain's Sanchez 'international order crumbling.' FM Guo: blockade 'dangerous and irresponsible.' Lavrov arrives Beijing for coordination with Wang Yi. China positioning as alternative mediator to Pakistan/US. This is a geopolitical realignment in real time.
verificationNote: CNBC, Foreign Policy, NBC News, RFE/RL confirmed Xi statement and Lavrov visit.

text: Day 47 PM roundup: Oil settles Brent $94.79 WTI $91.28. Israel-Lebanon DC talks agree to further negotiations (first since 1993). Trump hints Round 2 'in next two days.' US sanctions waiver expires April 19. Xi Jinping first personal statement. S&P 500 +1.18% erases ALL war losses. First ICRC aid to Iran. CENTCOM blockade holds. 17-nation coalition backs talks. IRGC: Hormuz approach = ceasefire violation. Vance 'economic terrorism.' US 399 WIA. Gas $4.118.
verificationNote: Compiled from NPR, Bloomberg, CNBC, State Dept, Foreign Policy, Euronews, ICRC, Al Jazeera, Motley Fool, AAA.

text: BLOCKADE DAY 2: US destroyer intercepts 2 oil tankers leaving Chabahar port on Gulf of Oman side. CENTCOM confirms 6 ships total turned back in first 24 hours — no shots fired, no boardings. 5 of 6 were oil tankers. Blockade applies only to Iran-bound traffic. Non-Iranian Hormuz transits continue freely. This is the most significant peacetime naval interdiction since the Cuba quarantine.
verificationNote: BusinessWorld Online, NBC News, Jerusalem Post confirmed interdiction details.

text: TRUMP FOX BUSINESS: 'I think it's close to over, yeah. I view it as close to over.' Also: 'If I pulled up stakes it would take them 20 years to rebuild.' NOT thinking about extending ceasefire (expires April 21). Hints Round 2 Pakistan talks 'in next two days.' But CNN official: 'nothing scheduled.' Mixed signals. Oil rebounds modestly: Brent $95.72 (+1.0%), WTI $91.50 (+0.2%). API: US crude +6.1M bbl (8th straight build).
verificationNote: Fox Business, CNN, CNBC, OilPriceAPI confirmed statements and prices.

text: IRAN DEMANDS $270 BILLION in war compensation from US, Israel, and 5 Arab states. Government spokesperson describes figure as 'very crude' preliminary calculation. Pezeshkian: blockade 'doomed to failure.' Interior Minister Momeni instructs governors to 'neutralize the threat.' Araghchi: came to talks 'in good faith' but met with 'maximalism and shifting goalposts.' Iran rejecting US 20-year enrichment moratorium; countered with 5-year (also rejected).
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, ANI News, Times of Israel confirmed Iran demands and statements.

text: GUTERRES: 'There is no military solution to this crisis. Serious negotiations must resume.' Thanks Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Egypt, Türkiye mediators. Switzerland reiterates Geneva offer. Xi-Lavrov confirmed meeting in Beijing — Lavrov says Russia could 'compensate' China's energy shortages. Pope Leo: 'I have no fear of the Trump administration.' Trump refuses to apologize after calling Pope 'WEAK on Crime.' Italy's Meloni calls Trump criticism 'unacceptable.'
verificationNote: UN News, Bloomberg, NBC News, Euronews confirmed all items.

text: Hezbollah fires 30+ rockets at northern Israel this morning (Times of Israel liveblog). 1 man injured. Meanwhile Bint Jbeil battle continues — IDF 98th Division encirclement nearing completion. IDF Northern Command Chief visited frontline yesterday. 10 soldiers wounded overnight in clashes. Armored battalion commander seriously injured. Bianco KIA confirmed (Humvee accident). 13 IDF soldiers killed in Lebanon since Feb 28.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog, Jerusalem Post, Antiwar.com confirmed operations and casualties.

text: Day 47 AM roundup: Oil rebounds Brent $95.72 WTI $91.50. Trump: 'close to over,' won't extend ceasefire. US destroyer intercepts 2 tankers Chabahar. Iran demands $270B compensation. Pezeshkian: blockade 'doomed to failure.' Guterres: 'no military solution.' Xi-Lavrov Beijing. Hezbollah 30+ rockets at north. Switzerland offers Geneva. Araghchi: 'good faith' met 'maximalism.' API: crude +6.1M bbl. Gas $4.118 flat.

text: BLOCKADE DAY 2 UPDATE: CENTCOM says blockade now "fully implemented" — 8 tankers intercepted (up from 6). Iran sea trade "completely halted." Iranian tanker with 2M barrels arrived unannounced near Gujarat, India — refiners may refuse it. 157.7M bbl Iranian oil floating. Trump: "permanently opening" Hormuz. 10,000+ US personnel enforcing. Sanctions waiver expires April 19 — 4 days.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, JNS, Voice of Emirates confirmed CENTCOM announcement and 8-tanker figure.

text: IRAN RED SEA THREAT: Maj. Gen. Abdollahi (Khatam al-Anbiya) threatens Iran will block ALL shipping in Persian Gulf, Sea of Oman, AND Red Sea if US blockade continues. First time Iran explicitly threatens Red Sea — would need Houthi proxies. This is a massive escalation in rhetoric. Meanwhile Trump posts: "permanently opening" Hormuz, claims China agreed no weapons to Iran. Two maximalist positions colliding.
verificationNote: Newsweek, NBC News, Middle East Eye, PressTV confirmed Abdollahi statement. Business Today India confirmed Trump post.

text: MEDIATION PUSH: Pakistan army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir lands in Tehran with Interior Minister Naqvi. Carries Washington message. Trump tells NY Post talks "over next two days" — called back to say "more likely" thanks to Munir. Netanyahu i24 interview endorses Greater Israel vision — "historic and spiritual mission." Gofman approved Mossad chief (no intel background). Mixed signals: mediation urgency vs maximalist posturing.
verificationNote: Bloomberg, UPI confirmed Munir Tehran arrival. Times of Israel, Middle East Eye confirmed Netanyahu Greater Israel.

text: LEBANON CONTINUES TO BURN: Israeli strikes kill 13+ in southern Lebanon — Jbaa (4 killed, family home), Ansariyeh (5), Qadmus (4). One day after historic DC talks. IDF struck 200+ Hezbollah targets in 24h. Hezbollah responds with 70 rockets at northern Israel (Nahariya targeted, 2 injured). Qassem calls talks "free concession." Ali Nikzad (Iran parliament): revenge vow for Khamenei. Total Lebanon toll: 2,137 killed, 6,921 wounded.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Washington Times, Times of Israel confirmed Lebanon strikes and Hezbollah rockets.

text: Day 47 PM roundup: Iran threatens Red Sea/Gulf total blockade (Abdollahi). Trump "permanently opening" Hormuz — China no-weapons deal. Pakistan army chief Munir in Tehran. CENTCOM: blockade "fully implemented" 8 tankers. Israel kills 13 in Lebanon. Netanyahu Greater Israel. Hezbollah 70 rockets. IMF: 3.1% growth, recession warning. EIA: Q2 Brent peak $115.
